operating_state: Total revenue increased 2.7% to $8.19 billion driven by 5.9 million net subscriber additions reaching 238.4 million global paid memberships, with strength across Asia-Pacific and Latin America while North American growth remained modest. Revenue per membership declined 13% year-over-year to $7.84 due to foreign exchange headwinds and higher growth in lower-priced regions, with constant currency decline of 6%.
cost_structure: Operating margin expanded 200 basis points to 22% as revenue growth outpaced expense increases. Cost of revenues remained flat representing 57% of revenue, down from 59% in prior year driven by content amortization efficiency. Technology and development expenses declined 8% due to personnel cost reductions, while marketing expenses increased 9% from higher advertising spend.
material_events: Netflix repurchased $645 million of common stock during Q2 2023 under the $5 billion authorization with $3.4 billion remaining available. The WGA strike commenced May 2023 followed by SAG-AFTRA strike beginning July 2023, causing production pauses and delayed content payments. The company announced plans to discontinue its DVD-by-mail service and amended its credit facility to replace LIBOR with Term SOFR.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency exposure intensified with 57% of revenue denominated in non-USD currencies, primarily euro, British pound, Brazilian real, and Japanese yen. Netflix recognized $130 million foreign exchange losses in the first half of 2023 compared to $431 million gains in prior year, driven by euro-denominated debt remeasurement. Content obligations of $20.9 billion include significant exposure to foreign currency fluctuations.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued significant investment in global original content with content obligations totaling $20.9 billion including $13.7 billion not yet recognized on balance sheets. Future debt market needs anticipated to be more limited compared to prior years. Strike-related production delays may increase variability in content payment timing while the company maintains focus on global business growth within operating margin targets.
uncertainty_profile: Content production remains subject to ongoing industry-wide labor strikes affecting timing and costs of future content investments. The company maintains $240 million in unrecognized tax benefits under IRS examination that could significantly change within twelve months. Foreign exchange volatility continues to create unpredictable impacts on revenue and earnings given substantial international revenue exposure and euro-denominated debt obligations.
